ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC used as closest defensible category for mixed vegetable salad with vinaigrette dressing; no exact Malay Selada entry exists in FoodEx2.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Selada is approximately 100 g, equivalent to a small side-salad bowl served alongside rice and meat dishes at a Malay meal or festive occasion.

nutrient_source.title: Vegetables and Vegetable Products — Raw Lettuce, Tomato, Cucumber composite; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or IMR Malaysia MyFCD entry exists for Malay Selada as a composite dish. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for raw lettuce (FDC ID 169247), raw tomato (FDC ID 170457), and raw cucumber (FDC ID 168409), weighted to reflect a typical Selada composition (approximately 50% lettuce, 25% tomato, 25% cucumber) with a light sugar-vinegar dressing contributing additional carbohydrate and sodium. Sodium value of 180 mg per 100 g reflects added vinaigrette dressing salt; this is plausible for a dressed salad. All values cross-checked against IMR Malaysia MyFCD vegetable entries where available.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Malay Selada. GI estimated at 20 based on analogous raw mixed-vegetable salad entries in the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values of 15–25 for raw leafy green and cucumber-tomato salads. The acidity of the vinegar-based dressing further suppresses postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 100 g serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (20 × 8.5) / 100 = 1.7, classified as very low.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium from vinaigrette dressing; high potassium from fresh vegetables
hypertension.guidance: Selada provides 160 mg potassium and 20 mg calcium per 100 g, which support vascular health; however, the vinaigrette dressing contributes approximately 180 mg sodium per 100 g, so individuals with hypertension should request reduced-salt dressing or limit portion size to manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~20) and low glycemic load (~1.7 per serving); sugar in dressing warrants monitoring
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of approximately 20 and a glycemic load of 1.7 per 100 g serving, Selada has a minimal impact on postprandial blood glucose; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should nonetheless be aware that the sugar added to the vinaigrette dressing contributes approximately 6 g of sugar per 100 g and should moderate dressing quantity accordingly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low fat (0.1 g per 100 g), zero cholesterol, and antioxidant vitamins A, C, and E from fresh vegetables
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Selada is very low in total fat (0.1 g per 100 g), contains no cholesterol, and provides antioxidant nutrients including vitamin A (65 mcg RAE), vitamin C (15 mg), and vitamin E (0.3 mg) per 100 g, making it a cardioprotective side dish; the primary cardiovascular consideration is the sodium content from the dressing, which should be minimised for individuals with established cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory antioxidants from fresh greens and tomatoes
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The vitamin C, vitamin K (45 mcg per 100 g), and folate content of Selada support immune and connective tissue health, and the dish is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis as a low-calorie, anti-inflammatory side.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Selada is a traditional Malay green salad commonly served as a side dish at Malay weddings, festive occasions, and everyday lunches and dinners in Malaysia and Singapore. The dish typically consists of fresh lettuce, sliced tomatoes, and cucumber, tossed in a light, tart vinaigrette made from vinegar, sugar, and optionally a hint of chili or lime juice. The result is a crisp, refreshing salad with a sweet-sour flavour profile that acts as a palate cleanser alongside rich meat dishes, rice, and curries. Selada is minimally processed, entirely plant-based, and free from common allergens. Nutritionally, it is very low in energy (approximately 35 kcal per 100 g), very low in fat (0.1 g), and provides moderate carbohydrate (8.5 g) largely from the sugar in the dressing. It is a good source of vitamin K (45 mcg), vitamin A (65 mcg RAE), vitamin C (15 mg), and potassium (160 mg) per 100 g. Its glycemic index is estimated at approximately 20 (low), making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. The sodium content of approximately 180 mg per 100 g from the vinaigrette dressing is the primary clinical consideration for sodium-sensitive individuals. Selada is vegan, vegetarian, halal, and gluten-free.
common_user_queries: What is in a Malay salad?
What is the glycemic index of Malay salad?
What is served at a Malay wedding?
meal_context: palate cleanser alongside nasi lemak or rendang
low-calorie addition to a rice-based meal

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Malay Selada as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for raw lettuce, tomato, and cucumber with a light sugar-vinegar dressing, cross-checked against IMR Malaysia MyFCD vegetable data. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for raw mixed-vegetable salads. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact match was found in any primary approved source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (raw lettuce FDC ID 169247, raw tomato FDC ID 170457, raw cucumber FDC ID 168409) weighted to a typical Selada composition and cross-checked against IMR Malaysia MyFCD. No exact Selada entry exists in any single approved source. 2. Sodium value of 180 mg per 100 g reflects the contribution of the sugar-vinegar dressing with added salt; this is plausible for a dressed salad but may vary significantly depending on preparation. 3. water_g set to -1: detailed moisture data for the composite dressed salad is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 and vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: these are naturally zero for a plant-based salad with no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central raw vegetable entries. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0 and cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero for a plant-based dish with no animal fats. 6. GI estimated at 20 from analogous raw mixed-vegetable salad entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Malay Selada exists in the published literature. 7. The input source (Dietitians Association of Australia) is not on the approved source list and has been replaced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as the primary nutrient source. 8. Ontology mapped to FoodOn FOODON_03301710 (salad food product) and FoodEx2 A00LC (salads and similar mixed vegetable dishes) as closest defensible approximations; no exact codes exist for Malay Selada.
